title: callitriche palustris var. stellata
intro: Callitriche palustris var. stellata, also known as starwort or aquatic spring whitlow-grass, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Callitriche palustris var. stellata include its ability to relieve congestion, reduce fever, and soothe sore throats. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
